reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails the fundamental criteria of Content Accuracy. The user explicitly requested the text '每个字，都要读清楚。' (Every character must be read clearly). However, Model 1 generated completely unrelated content: '现在北京时间是二十一时十八分' (Current Beijing time is 21:18). According to the Global Considerations and Scoring Standard, if the voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script, the score must be 0, regardless of the speech rate or audio quality.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 follows the instructions perfectly. First, the Content Accuracy is perfect; it reads exactly '每个字，都要读清楚。'. Second, regarding the target feature '语速' (Speech Rate), it strictly adheres to the command 'extremely slow' (极慢速). The model articulates each character with significant spacing and deliberate elongation (e.g., '每...个...字...'), spanning the short phrase over 8 seconds. This perfectly satisfies the requirement to read every character clearly at an extremely slow pace. The prosody is controlled and fits the specific 'dictation-style' request.
model_comparison: There is no contest here due to Model 1's hallucination of the text. Model 1 ignored the input text and generated a time announcement, resulting in a score of 0. Model 2 correctly produced the text and excellently implemented the 'extremely slow' speech rate constraint, separating every syllable distinctly as requested. Model 2 is the superior model.
